rationale.summary: The walkthrough says this PR replaces the old per-second log rate limiter with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates sampler configuration to clients, and rewrites access logging through lazy typed facades. It also updates deployment documentation/configuration from a single log-rate-limit knob to request, access, and diagnostic sample-rate knobs, and adds sampler-focused tests. No conversation transcript was present, so human intent could not be independently matched against the walkthrough.

keyPoints.point: The PR replaces the old limiter with a worker-authoritative random sampler and client propagation.
keyPoints.snippet: This PR replaces the old per-second log rate limiter with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates sampling config to clients

keyPoints.point: Deployment configuration is renamed from one rate-limit knob to three sampling-rate knobs.
keyPoints.snippet: Deployment and operator docs switch from the single per-second `log_rate_limit` knob to three decimal sample-rate knobs.

keyPoints.point: Access logging call sites move to lazy typed facades to avoid eager metadata construction.
keyPoints.snippet: C, C++, Java, and Python client paths are moved from eager `RequestParam` assembly to the typed access recorder facade.

keyPoints.point: Tests are refocused around sampler config, integration, access facades, and performance guardrails.
keyPoints.snippet: Unit tests remove the old rate-limiter suite and add coverage for sampler config, access sampling, integration with log macros, and typed access facades.

critique_ledger.title: Runtime sampler flag transaction has no regression test
critique_ledger.rationale: The new UpdateFlagParameter path batches request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, and diagnostic_sample_rate through validation and commit logic, but the diff only changes an unrelated flags_test assertion indentation, so regressions in sampler updates, derivation, or invalid-batch rollback would not be caught.

critique_ledger.title: Sampler flag name set is duplicated across update paths
critique_ledger.rationale: The same three sampler flag names are declared in multiple update paths, so future additions, renames, or removals can silently split validation from dispatch behavior.

critique_ledger.title: Batch delete access logging now copies every object key
critique_ledger.rationale: DeleteAllCopy previously logged a bounded key summary, but the new ObjectKeysRef path materializes every protobuf key into a vector before formatting, adding O(number of keys) heap allocations and string copies on the worker request path when sampled in.

critique_ledger.title: Config snapshot retirement leaks memory across dynamic updates
critique_ledger.rationale: Every sampler config change appends the old snapshot to oldSnapshots_, and production code only frees that vector during Shutdown, so repeated dynamic updates can retain one snapshot per change for the process lifetime.

critique_ledger.title: Duplicate object-key formatting logic for protobuf keys
critique_ledger.rationale: The protobuf overload repeats truncation, count, and total-size logic from the vector overload, making future access-log key formatting changes easy to miss in one implementation.

critique_ledger.title: Comment relies on a brittle design-document line reference
critique_ledger.rationale: The zero-rate branch points to a design-document line number instead of explaining the local invariant, so the reference can drift and future maintainers must chase external context before modifying the branch.

routed_questions.Documentation and deployment config: Does the changed deployment/config hunk provide a compatibility path or explicit migration guidance for existing users of `log_rate_limit` and `DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT`?
routed_questions.Sampler core and propagation: Does the changed sampler/config propagation hunk preserve rollback, derivation, and memory-retirement safety across dynamic sampler updates?

per_cohort.routed_question: Does the changed deployment/config hunk provide a compatibility path or explicit migration guidance for existing users of `log_rate_limit` and `DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT`?
Does the changed sampler/config propagation hunk preserve rollback, derivation, and memory-retirement safety across dynamic sampler updates?

reasons: L4 (Cleanroom) pending for cohort(s): Documentation and deployment config, Sampler core and propagation
